name: Social Cognition and Object Relations Scale - Global Rating Method
authors: Drew Westen (original); Stein, Hilsenroth, Slavin-Mulford, Pinsker (SCORS-G)
source: Stein & Slavin-Mulford (2018). The SCORS-G: A Comprehensive Guide. Routledge.

dimensions.name: Complexity of Representations of People
dimensions.definition: Evaluates the degree of differentiation of self and other representations, including awareness of internal states and level of relational enmeshment or differentiation.

scale.anchor: Egocentric; sometimes confuses thoughts, feelings, or attributes of the self and others (boundary confusion, merger, lack of differentiation)
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Describes people's personalities and internal states in minimally elaborated, relatively simplistic ways; may split representations into all-good and all-bad
Intermediate between 3 and 5
Representations of self and others are stereotypical or conventional; able to integrate both positive and negative characteristics; has awareness of impact on others
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Psychologically minded; shows insight into self and others; representations are differentiated and show considerable complexity

dimensions.name: Affective Quality of Representations
dimensions.definition: Assesses expectations from relationships and how one experiences significant others — the overall emotional tone of the interpersonal world.

scale.anchor: Malevolent, abusive, caustic (the world is experienced as dangerous and hostile; people are seen as threatening, cruel, or destructive)
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Largely negative or unpleasant, but not abusive (relationships are disappointing, frustrating, empty, but not actively threatening)
Bland, absent, or limited affective quality (neutral/no clear emotional valence)
Mixed, neither primarily positive nor primarily negative (must include some positive to warrant this score)
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Generally positive expectations of relationships; a favorable and affirmative view of relationships (but not pollyannaish)

dimensions.name: Emotional Investment in Relationships
dimensions.definition: Depth of emotional commitment in relationships; extent to which others are treated as ends rather than means.

scale.anchor: Focuses primarily on own needs in relationships; has tumultuous relationships, or has few if any relationships (others are tools for need gratification)
Only one character; no relationship depicted
Somewhat shallow relationships, or only alludes to others (superficial connections without depth)
Intermediate between 3 and 5
Demonstrates conventional sentiments of friendship, caring, love, and empathy
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Deep, committed relationships with mutual sharing, emotional intimacy, interdependence, and respect; positive connectedness and appreciation of others

dimensions.name: Emotional Investment in Values and Moral Standards
dimensions.definition: Degree of internalization and application of moral values; the maturity of moral reasoning.

scale.anchor: Evidences a relative absence of moral values and concerns for others; may behave in selfish, inconsiderate, self-indulgent, or aggressive ways without remorse or guilt
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Shows signs of some internalization of standards (avoids doing 'bad' things because others will think badly); thinks in simple or childlike ways about right and wrong; may be rigid
No moral concerns raised in narrative (default when moral content absent)
Is invested in moral values and experiences guilt for hurting others or failing to meet moral standards; has conventional moral views; attempts to uphold them
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Thinks about moral questions combining abstract reasoning, willingness to challenge convention, and genuine compassion and thoughtfulness in actions

dimensions.name: Understanding of Social Causality
dimensions.definition: Coherence of understanding interpersonal events, attributions about why people act as they do, and psychological-mindedness about social interactions.

scale.anchor: Narrative accounts of interpersonal experiences are confused, distorted, extremely sparse, or difficult to follow; limited awareness and coherence
Alogical events lacking causal understanding (things happen but no explanation is given or can be inferred)
Understands people in relatively simple but sensible ways; describes interpersonal events that largely make sense but may have gaps or incongruities
Intermediate between 3 and 5
Tends to provide straightforward narrative accounts of interpersonal events in which people's actions result from how they experience or interpret situations
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Tends to provide particularly coherent narrative accounts of interpersonal events; understands people very well (shows sophisticated psychological-mindedness)

dimensions.name: Experience and Management of Aggressive Impulses
dimensions.definition: How anger and aggression are experienced, expressed, and controlled.

scale.anchor: Physically assaultive, destructive, sadistic, or in poor control of aggressive impulses; impulsive
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Tends to be angry, passive-aggressive, denigrating of others, physically abusive to self, or unable to protect self from escapable abuse
No anger content in narrative (default when aggressive themes absent)
Avoids dealing with anger by denying it, defending against it, or avoiding confrontations
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Can express anger and aggression and assert themselves appropriately (anger is modulated and used constructively)

dimensions.name: Self-Esteem
dimensions.definition: Overall feelings about oneself; the valence and stability of self-regard.

scale.anchor: Views self as loathsome, evil, rotten, contaminating, or globally bad
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Has low self-esteem (feels inadequate, inferior, self-critical), or is unrealistically grandiose
Self-esteem is bland, absent, or limited (default when self-evaluation not salient)
Displays a range of positive and negative feelings toward the self (must include some positive to warrant this score)
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Tends to have realistically positive feelings about themselves

dimensions.name: Identity and Coherence of Self
dimensions.definition: Stability and integration of the self-concept; the sense of who one is over time.

scale.anchor: Fragmented sense of self; has multiple personalities (extreme identity diffusion)
Intermediate between 1 and 3
Views of, or feelings about, the self fluctuate widely and unpredictably; unstable sense of self
Upward extension of level 3 (some ambiguity about goals is acceptable)
Identity and self-definition are not a major concern or preoccupation (stable but not elaborated)
Intermediate between 5 and 7
Feels like an integrated person with long-term ambitions and goals

decision_rules.default_scores: For AFF, EIM, AGG, SE: score 4 when relevant content is absent from the narrative
decision_rules.even_scores: Intermediate ratings between odd-numbered anchor points; no half-points
decision_rules.scoring_unit: Each narrative is scored independently; scores are averaged across all narratives in a protocol
